hazard.summary: CI VLAB switches never become ready and readiness wait hits context deadline exceeded; root cause is intermittent control-plane reachability failure caused by e1000 NIC Tx Unit Hang (SSH/show-tech also times out).
signals: Switches status notReady="[leaf-01 leaf-02 leaf-03 leaf-04 leaf-05 spine-01 spine-02]"
Failed to wait for ready err="waiting for ready: context deadline exceeded"
Some diagnostics collection failed ... ssh ... connect failed (Connection timed out)
kernel: e1000 ... Detected Tx Unit Hang
Destination Host Unreachable (ping to control-plane API IP)
control-plane API unreachable from switch console

constraints: Readiness waits must surface the underlying dependency failure (control-plane reachability) rather than only a generic context deadline exceeded.
CI/VLAB networking must avoid known-bad NIC driver behavior (e1000 Tx Unit Hang) or apply mitigations that prevent Tx stalls.
Diagnostics collection must have a console/alternate path when SSH/proxy is unavailable, and must clearly report which path failed and why.
Timeouts/retries must be bounded and paired with a fast classification signature so repeated flakes are diagnosed in minutes, not hours.
knobs: nic_driver: e1000 vs virtio (VLAB control VM)
verification: Reproduce by running VLAB CI jobs that previously hit notReady≥15x and context deadline exceeded; confirm signature disappears after mitigation.
Confirm control-plane API reachability from switch console during the test (ping/API health) remains stable across runs.
Confirm control VM no longer logs e1000 "Detected Tx Unit Hang" (or that virtio migration/offload-disable prevents Tx hangs).
Confirm diagnostics collection succeeds via SSH or console fallback and the collected show-tech includes enough data to classify future failures.
notes: This receipt captures the evolved understanding: the initial symptom (switches notReady → deadline exceeded) masked the real failure (control-plane reachability loss driven by e1000 Tx hang). The remediation path discussed includes disabling remaining offloads and/or migrating to virtio; console show-tech fallback is part of making the failure classifiable under SSH outages.
